{{Infobox event
| name = Incarnation
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Incarnation Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|An Incarnation is a being that has become the physical embodiment of its specific [[Territory]]'s [[essence]] and power. This transformation occurs when a [[Traveler]] draws an excessive amount of their [[Territory]]'s energy into themselves, upsetting the natural balance. Once this process is complete, the individual is no longer human and becomes a fundamental force of nature, similar to gravity or heat. [[Incarnations]] possess immense, nearly limitless power and access to the abilities of their respective Houses. However, they are inherently tied to their home worlds; if they remain in a foreign realm for too long, the environment begins to warp and reshape itself to mirror their [[Territory]]. This phenomenon can lead to an Incarnation becoming trapped within their own world as they are absorbed into its fabric. While some view them as noble creatures, others see them as dangerous, rabid beasts that must be chained or sealed away to prevent destruction. Maintaining these seals often requires gruesome blood sacrifices to the [[Trees]] of Ragnarus. The transition into an Incarnation is a volatile period where the individual's will is temporarily lost to an overwhelming compulsion to act according to their [[Territory]]'s nature, making them extremely unpredictable and dangerous. While some [[Incarnations]] may retain their personality or embody specific virtues, they are fundamentally defined by the nature of the world they represent.}}

== House of Blades ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|If the chains cover a person's body, it leads to [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13">''House of Blades'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] wishes to avoid risking [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c18">''House of Blades'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|An Incarnation is a force of nature, similar to gravity or heat, that serves as an embodiment of its [[Territory]]'s power and nature. This entity is a part of [[Valinhall]] that can warp reality to reform the world into its own [[Territory]], though entering such a space results in being trapped. Arising when a [[Traveler]] loses control due to black chain-marks, an Incarnation can summon monster armies and has previously attacked the [[Damascans]]. During a battle within the corridors of a house, this combatant wielded a flame-headed hammer against [[Simon]]. While [[Alin]] doubted his ability to assist against such full power, [[Simon]] required a mask to face the entity, knowing that if the chains fully wrapped around his neck, he would reach Incarnation.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">18</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Incarnations]] can warp the world around them to reform reality into their [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Incarnations]] are kept enslaved by a system that pays lives to maintain it.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Incarnation]] is the process where a [[Traveler]] loses control of their power due to being covered by black chain-marks.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] has attacked the [[Damascans]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Incarnations]] become trapped if they enter their own [[Territories]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] is located within the corridors of the house.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] is the incarnation of the world.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|An [[Incarnation]] is a force, like heat or gravity.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|An [[Incarnation]] is an embodiment of their [[Territory]]'s nature and power.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Incarnations]] can summon an army of monsters from their [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] found two survivors of the [[Dragon Army]] slaughter worthy.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c18">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] needs the mask to defeat the [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] is a part of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|If the chains wrap fully around [[Simon]]'s neck, he will reach [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] is a combatant in the battle.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] was battling [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] was unsure of his ability to help against the full power of an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Incarnation]] possesses a flame-headed hammer.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|An Incarnation is a creature that occurs when a [[Traveler]] draws excessive power from their [[Territory]], a transformation considered a death sentence. These entities are exceptions to many rules and are characterized by dark chains upon their skin and eyes where darkness bleeds into the whites. While they can be killed or sealed, an Incarnation does not return after death, but instead leaves the way free for another to take its place. [[Alin]] is an Incarnation, and it has been noted that [[Zakareth]] could see the benefits of such a state. During the events in [[the House]], an Incarnation composed of clockwork components beneath its armor caused widespread destruction. Additionally, the [[Ornheim Incarnation]] utilized summoned golems to combat [[Kathrin]] and [[Andra]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">15</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] could see the benefits of [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]]'s mission is to fight an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnations]] are exceptions to many rules.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnations]] do not return when they are killed; instead, they leave the way free for another to [[Incarnate]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5">''City of Light'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|An [[Incarnation]]'s eyes are characterized by darkness bleeding into the whites.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|An [[Incarnation]] has dark chains on their skin.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnations]] can be sealed or killed.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Incarnation]] is wrecking [[the House]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Incarnation]] is a creature with clockwork components beneath its armor.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] considers calling [[steel]] to turn into an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnation]] happens to [[Travelers]] who draw too much of their [[Territory]]'s power into themselves.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c18">''City of Light'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was seen fighting what may or may not have been an [[Incarnation]] before he vanished.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Being transformed into an [[Incarnation]] is considered a death sentence.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Ornheim Incarnation]] summoned golems to fight [[Kathrin]] and [[Andra]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Do not let the chains cover your body,” he said. “What? Why?” Simon asked, shaken. The chains were already up past his elbows; how much longer did he have? “What will happen if they do?” “Incarnation,” Kai responded.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“The man and the Incarnation of our world, each with his own goals.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“Without the mask, I can’t defeat the Incarnation.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Events]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate events]]
